title: The Wound I Dared to Touch
narration: Eight days have passed since they first told me.
I would not believe it then. Not from malice — only from ignorance.
I had said it plainly: unless I see the marks of the nails in his hands,
and put my finger into the place of the nails, and my hand into his side — I will not believe.
Now we are gathered again, inside, on Mount Sion. The doors are shut. This time I am with them.
Then he comes. The good shepherd, anxious for one small lamb of his little flock.
The doors stay closed — yet he is here. He stands in the midst of us, where every eye can reach him.
Peace be to you, he says.
And then — before I have asked — he answers the question I never spoke aloud.
Put in your finger here, he says. See my hands.
Bring your hand, and put it into my side.
He knows my words. He was not here when I spoke them, yet he knows. In this he shows me he is God.
Reach into the place of the nails. Reach into the wound the lance has opened.
The side asks not a finger but a whole hand — so great is that wound.
And know that I am the same who hung upon the cross.
Do not be unbelieving — slow to believe — but faithful, firm in faith.
For in the sin of unbelief, he says, you crucify me again, you renew the cause of my Passion.
I touch the scars. My hand goes into that side — that sweetest side, that side of salvation,
whence we are saved from wrath, healed of guilt, reborn in grace, raised into glory.
O the boldness of my hand! What licence, what grace was given me!
And I cry out — not in heart alone but with my mouth, for both are needed for salvation —
My Lord and my God.
My Lord, in your manhood, in which you redeemed me with your blood;
my God, in your Godhead, in which you made me, formed me from the dust.
Now I doubt nothing. I am certain. I confirm the Resurrection; I proclaim immortality.
And he answers, blessing my confession: Because you have seen me, Thomas, you have believed.
Blessed are they who have not seen, and have believed.
I, who am called Didymus for my doubting, double heart — I have touched the deep of his divinity.
prayer: Lord Jesus, peace which you brought in coming and left in departing,
do not leave me in my unbelief, as you did not leave Thomas.
Let whatever is discernment and work in me be spent in your service —
my finger and my hand laid in the place of the nails.
Make me not slow to believe, but firm in faith,
so that I crucify you no more by doubting.
And though I do not see you with my eyes, let me believe you with my heart,
that I may be among the blessed who have not seen, and yet have believed.
My Lord and my God. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: O felix Thoma, quam felicem habuit licentiam manus tua!
latinJewel.english: O happy Thomas, what a happy licence your hand was given!
